## Security & Compliance

> ⚠️ LOCKED — These rules cannot be overridden.

### Secrets & Credentials

#### No hardcoded secrets
NEVER commit API keys, passwords, tokens, or connection strings in code. Flag patterns: `sk_live_`, `AKIA`, `-----BEGIN RSA`, `password=`, `secret=`. ALWAYS reference secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Doppler). Block any PR containing literal secrets.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Injection Prevention

#### Parameterized queries only
NEVER use string concatenation, template literals, or f-strings in SQL/NoSQL/LDAP/OS commands with user input. ALWAYS use parameterized queries ($1, ?, :param), prepared statements, or ORM bindings. Example: use `db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1', [userId])` instead of `query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + userId)`.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Data Privacy & PII

#### Never disable TLS/SSL verification
NEVER set `rejectUnauthorized: false`, `verify=False`, `InsecureSkipVerify: true`, or `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0`. Flag as CRITICAL if found in any HTTP client, database connection, or API call. Use proper certificate chains; for local dev, use mkcert for locally-trusted certificates.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

#### Never log sensitive data
NEVER log passwords, API keys, tokens, session IDs, credit cards, SSNs, emails, or phone numbers. Flag patterns like `console.log(user)`, `logger.info(request.body)`, `JSON.stringify(patient)`. Safe to log: internal UUIDs, request IDs, action descriptions, error codes, timestamps. Logs are broadly accessible and retained long-term.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

#### Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit
NEVER store passwords in plaintext or use MD5/SHA for password hashing. MUST use TLS 1.2+ for all network communication, AES-256+ for storage encryption, and bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 for passwords. NEVER implement custom crypto—use only established libraries (crypto, libsodium). Verify all APIs and ciphersuites exist before use.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Authorization & Access Control

#### Authorization on every endpoint
MUST verify server-side that authenticated user is authorized for the specific resource. Flag missing ownership checks on `GET/PUT/DELETE /:id` and role verification on admin endpoints. NEVER rely on client-side guards alone. Prevent IDOR/BOLA vulnerabilities.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

#### Never implement custom authentication
NEVER build custom password hashing, JWT validation, session management, or OAuth flows. MUST use project's established auth system or proven provider (Auth0, Firebase, Cognito, Clerk). Only build auth if the project IS the auth service.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Destructive Operations

#### No unguarded destructive operations
NEVER execute DELETE, DROP, rm -rf, git push --force, or git reset --hard without explicit confirmation flag. MUST implement soft deletes and rollback scripts for all migrations. AI tools must never autonomously execute irreversible operations.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

#### Database changes through migrations only
MUST route all schema changes through migrations (Drizzle/Prisma/Alembic/Flyway). NEVER run ad-hoc SQL or direct ALTER TABLE outside migration files. Every migration MUST include rollback capability. Direct modifications bypass review and recovery.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Error Exposure

#### No internal details in error responses
NEVER expose stack traces, SQL, file paths, or internal IDs in API responses. MUST return `{ "error": { "code": "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE", "message": "User-safe text", "requestId": "..." } }`. Route all errors through centralized handler.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

## Architecture & Patterns

### Error Handling

#### No silent error swallowing
NEVER use empty catch blocks or bare `except: pass`. Every catch MUST handle (log with context, retry, or fallback), re-throw, or include a comment explaining why it's safe to ignore. Example: `catch(e) { logger.error('Failed to fetch user', {error: e}); return null; }`
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

### Testing

#### Tests required for new logic
MUST write tests for new functions, endpoints, and behavioral changes. ALWAYS include: happy path, one error case, boundary conditions. Place tests per project pattern. Untested code silently breaks during refactoring.
<!-- Source: canopy-mvp (org) -->

## Conventions
